prose:
Method–provenance summary
Property: HOMO–LUMO (frontier-orbital) gap, HOMO and LUMO energies (eV)
Level of theory: DFT, tier `standard` ⇒ B3LYP/def2-TZVP (RI-J) in PySCF 2.x
Geometry provenance: user-supplied fixed Cartesian coordinates (ethylene.xyz, 6 atoms); single-point calculation — no geometry relaxation (notation: DFT//user-provided)
Environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent)
Molecular charge: 0  |  multiplicity: 1 (closed-shell singlet, RHF/RKS reference)
SCF convergence: converged in 6 cycles (scf_converged = True)
Backend & version: PySCF (reported by chemkit)
Warnings: no warnings reported by the engine
Live log: the run was streamed to a .out file in the caller’s working directory (path given at launch via chemkit; retrievable for full SCF/DFT transcript)

Computed results (B3LYP/def2-TZVP, gas phase):
• Total electronic energy  E = –78.623486 Eh (–2139.4541 eV)
• HOMO energy            ε_H = –7.6155818 eV
• LUMO energy            ε_L = –0.17986527 eV
→ HOMO–LUMO gap          Δε   = 7.435716547018136 eV  (Koopmans, vertical)

All numbers are quoted as returned by chemkit without rounding. No experimental comparison requested; provenance is purely computational.
